records.description: Procedure for preparing the Institute's public annual report. Covers data gathering, financial summary, narrative drafting, design, Board approval, and publication.

steps.description: Gather metrics and highlights from all units: EduActive, ReInference, Administrative, and Communications.
steps.name: Data Collection

steps.description: Write the annual report narrative: President's letter, mission impact, research highlights, education outcomes, financial summary context.
steps.name: Narrative Drafting

steps.description: Prepare financial highlights: revenue by source, expense categories, program vs. administrative ratio, year-over-year trends.
steps.name: Financial Summary

steps.description: Design the annual report: apply brand guidelines, create infographics, lay out photos and charts, produce document and web versions.
steps.name: Design & Layout

steps.description: Present final report to Board for approval. Publish on website and distribute to stakeholders, donors, and regulatory filings.
steps.name: Board Approval & Publication

records.title: Annual Report Preparation

records.description: The formal process by which the Board of Directors reviews, deliberates, and votes on proposed policies. Governs the transition from PROPOSED → FOR_VOTE → ACCEPTED in the policy lifecycle.

steps.description: The policy author submits the completed draft to the Board Secretary for placement on the review agenda. The Secretary verifies completeness against the resolved template schema.
steps.name: Submit for Board Review

steps.description: Board members receive the review packet and have 14 days to read, comment, and submit questions. The policy status transitions to PROPOSED.
steps.name: Board Review Period

steps.description: The Board discusses the policy in a scheduled meeting. Amendments may be proposed and the policy may be sent back for revision or advanced to vote.
steps.name: Board Deliberation

steps.description: The Board conducts a formal vote on the policy. Requires a simple majority for adoption. The policy transitions to FOR_VOTE status during this step.
steps.name: Formal Vote

steps.description: Upon approval, the Secretary records the vote, updates the policy status to ACCEPTED, and publishes the ratified version to the policy library.
steps.name: Ratification & Publication

records.title: Board of Directors Policy Approval
triggers: Board meeting agenda includes policy vote
Emergency policy action requested by Officer

records.description: Procedure for registering and maintaining compliance for a foreign nonprofit operating in California. Covers Statement of Information, Attorney General registration, and Franchise Tax Board.

steps.description: File Statement and Designation by Foreign Corporation (Form S&DC) with the California Secretary of State. Fee: $30.
steps.name: Qualify as Foreign Corporation

steps.description: File Initial Registration Form (CT-1) with the CA Attorney General's Registry of Charitable Trusts within 30 days of receiving charitable assets. Fee: $25.
steps.name: Register with CA Attorney General

steps.description: File Form FTB 3500 with the California Franchise Tax Board to obtain state tax-exempt status. Requires IRS Determination Letter.
steps.name: Apply for CA Tax Exemption (FTB 3500)

steps.description: File Statement of Information with the CA Secretary of State within 90 days of fiscal year end. Fee: $20.
steps.name: File Annual Statement of Information (SI-100)

steps.description: File Annual Registration Renewal Fee Report and FTB Form 199 with the Attorney General. Due within 4 months and 15 days after fiscal year end.
steps.name: File Annual AG Renewal (RRF-1 + Form 199)

records.title: California Operations Registration & Compliance
triggers: Annual Statement of Information due (within 90 days of fiscal year end)
Attorney General annual renewal (RRF-1 due with Form 199)

records.description: Procedure for incorporating a nonprofit in Delaware and maintaining annual compliance. Covers Certificate of Incorporation, Registered Agent, Annual Report, and Franchise Tax.

steps.description: Draft Certificate of Incorporation for a Delaware Nonstock Corporation (Title 8, Chapter 1, §101-102). Include purpose clause with 501(c)(3) language.
steps.name: Prepare Certificate of Incorporation

steps.description: Submit Certificate of Incorporation to Delaware Division of Corporations. Pay filing fee ($89 for nonstock corporations).
steps.name: File with Delaware Division of Corporations

steps.description: Ensure a registered agent is appointed in Delaware at all times for service of process and state correspondence.
steps.name: Maintain Registered Agent

steps.description: File Annual Report with the Delaware Division of Corporations by March 1 each year. Fee: $25 for nonprofits.
steps.name: File Delaware Annual Report

steps.description: Non-profit corporations exempt from franchise tax under DE Code Title 8 §503. File annual exempt report confirming exempt status.
steps.name: Pay Delaware Franchise Tax

records.title: Delaware Incorporation & Annual Compliance

records.description: End-to-end procedure for establishing and maintaining federal tax-exempt status under IRC §501(c)(3). Covers IRS Form 1023 filing, EIN application, determination letter tracking, and ongoing compliance.

steps.description: Apply for an EIN online via IRS.gov (Form SS-4). Required before Form 1023 filing.
steps.name: Obtain Employer Identification Number (EIN)

steps.description: Compile the application for tax-exempt status: organizational documents, narrative description of activities, financial projections, board composition, and conflict-of-interest policy.
steps.name: Prepare Form 1023 / 1023-EZ Application

steps.description: File Form 1023/1023-EZ via Pay.gov with the user fee. Track confirmation number.
steps.name: Submit to IRS

steps.description: Monitor IRS processing (typically 3-6 months). Respond to any IRS follow-up questions.
steps.name: Determination Letter Tracking

steps.description: Update state registrations with exempt status, register with state charity regulators, set up donation receipting system, file initial Form 990.
steps.name: Post-Determination Setup

steps.description: Establish annual compliance calendar: Form 990, state renewals, public disclosure requirements, unrelated business income tracking.
steps.name: Annual Compliance Calendar

records.title: Federal 501(c)(3) Registration & Maintenance
triggers: Annual Form 990 filing deadline (May 15 for calendar year)
Material change in operations or governance

records.description: Procedure for detecting, containing, eradicating, and recovering from IT security incidents. Aligns with NIST CSF and the Institute's Incident Response & Breach Notification Policy.

steps.description: Identify the potential security incident through monitoring, alerting, or user report. Log the initial indicator of compromise (IoC).
steps.name: Detection & Alerting

steps.description: Assess the scope, severity (P1–P4), and potential impact. Determine if breach notification is required under GDPR/CCPA.
steps.name: Triage & Classification

steps.description: Isolate affected systems, revoke compromised credentials, and prevent further spread. Short-term and long-term containment strategies.
steps.name: Containment

steps.description: Remove the root cause — malware, unauthorized access, misconfiguration. Patch vulnerable systems.
steps.name: Eradication

steps.description: Restore affected systems from clean backups, validate integrity, and return to normal operations.
steps.name: Recovery

steps.description: Conduct a blameless post-mortem. Document root cause, timeline, impact, and preventive measures. Update policies if needed.
steps.name: Post-Mortem & Lessons Learned

records.title: IT Security Incident Response

records.description: Procedure for filing and paying annual organizational taxes for a 501(c)(3) nonprofit registered in Delaware and active in California (e.g., IRS Form 990, CA Form 199, DE Franchise Tax).

steps.name: CPA Preparation

steps.description: Distribute IRS Form 990 to the full Board of Directors for review prior to filing, as recommended by IRS governance guidelines.
steps.name: Board Review

steps.description: Authorize CPA to electronically file IRS Form 990 (or 990-EZ/990-N).
steps.name: File Federal Returns (IRS)

steps.description: File Delaware Annual Report and California FTB Form 199 + Attorney General Form RRF-1.
steps.name: File State Returns (DE & CA)

steps.description: Remit any required payments or filing fees (e.g., DE Franchise Tax, CA Registry of Charitable Trusts fee).
steps.name: Payment Remittance

records.title: Pay Organizational Taxes
triggers: Approaching statutory tax deadlines (e.g., May 15 for calendar year nonprofits)

records.description: Meta-process governing the full lifecycle of any policy document from initial exploration through retirement. Maps directly to the 8 board statuses defined in board_statuses.json.

steps.description: Identify the need for a policy. Research peer organizations, regulatory requirements, and stakeholder needs. Produce a scoping brief.
steps.name: Explore & Scope

steps.description: Compose the policy using the resolved template schema standard. Collaborate with subject matter experts and AI drafting assistants.
steps.name: Draft Policy

steps.description: Submit the completed draft to the Board for formal review. Status transitions from DRAFTING to PROPOSED.
steps.name: Submit Proposal

steps.description: Revise the draft based on Board feedback. Track amendments and re-submit. Status transitions to AMENDED.
steps.name: Amend (if needed)

steps.description: Invoke the Board Policy Approval process (board-policy-approval). Status transitions to FOR_VOTE.
steps.name: Board Vote

steps.description: Policy is accepted and published to the library. Status transitions to ACCEPTED.
steps.name: Accept & Publish

steps.description: Ongoing monitoring of the active policy. Periodic reviews triggered by schedule or regulatory changes.
steps.name: Monitor & Review

steps.description: When a policy is no longer needed or is replaced, it transitions to SUPERSEDED or RETIRED.
steps.name: Retire or Supersede

records.title: Policy Lifecycle Management
triggers: Board directive to create or retire a policy

records.description: Procedure for proposing, scoping, and launching new research projects. Ensures alignment with mission, resource availability, and proper governance.

steps.description: Researcher submits a 2-page project proposal: objectives, methodology (Active Inference framing), expected outcomes, timeline, and resource needs.
steps.name: Proposal Submission

steps.description: Scientific Advisory Board evaluates methodological rigor, novelty, and alignment with Active Inference principles.
steps.name: Scientific Merit Review

steps.description: Determine required compute, personnel, data access, and budget. President approval required for projects exceeding $5,000.
steps.name: Resource Allocation

steps.description: Review intellectual property implications, data privacy requirements, and ethical considerations. Ensure compliance with open science mission.
steps.name: Ethics & IP Review

steps.description: Kickoff meeting with project team: review scope, timeline, milestones, communication plan, and deliverables. Create GitHub repository.
steps.name: Project Kickoff

records.title: Research Project Initiation

records.description: Procedure for evaluating, interviewing, and selecting candidates for the Research Fellow program.

steps.description: Receive the application materials (CV, cover letter, research proposal) and log them into the applicant tracking system.
steps.name: Application Intake

steps.description: Screen application for completeness and basic qualifications against the fellowship criteria.
steps.name: Initial Screening

steps.description: Scientific Advisory Board or ReInference Director reviews the research proposal for alignment with Active Inference methodology.
steps.name: Scientific Review

steps.description: Conduct a panel interview with the candidate to assess technical skills, cultural fit, and communication.
steps.name: Interview Panel

steps.description: President or ReInference Director makes the final selection decision based on all accumulated evidence.
steps.name: Final Decision

steps.description: Extend offer to candidate. Upon acceptance, trigger the employee-onboarding process.
steps.name: Offer & Onboarding

records.title: Review of Research Fellow Application

records.description: Procedure for evaluating, approving, and onboarding third-party vendors and service providers. Ensures compliance with procurement, risk management, and data protection requirements.

steps.description: Internal stakeholder submits a vendor engagement request with business justification, estimated spend, and data access requirements.
steps.name: Vendor Request

steps.description: Procurement Manager conducts due diligence: financial stability, references, compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001), ESG practices.
steps.name: Due Diligence Review

steps.description: Compliance Officer and DPO assess data protection risks, regulatory exposure, and vendor lock-in risk. Score using the Institute's risk matrix.
steps.name: Risk Assessment

steps.description: Negotiate contract terms, SLAs, data processing agreements (DPAs), and liability clauses. President approval required above spending thresholds.
steps.name: Contract Negotiation & Review

steps.description: Provision access, issue credentials, configure integrations, and add vendor to the entity registry. Schedule first review date.
steps.name: Vendor Onboarding

records.title: Vendor Due Diligence & Onboarding

steps.description: Establish the grant timeline, assign writing sections to contributors, and review funder guidelines.

steps.description: Write the core proposal narrative, including methodology, literature review, and expected outcomes.
steps.name: Draft Narrative

steps.description: Create the project budget and budget narrative, ensuring alignment with institutional financial controls.
steps.name: Develop Budget

steps.description: Scientific Advisory Board or peer reviewers evaluate the proposal for clarity, feasibility, and impact.
steps.name: Internal Review (Red Team)

steps.description: President and/or Board of Directors grant final sign-off before submission to the funder.
steps.name: Final Approval

steps.description: Compile all components and submit via the funder's portal before the deadline.
